analyzer.cache_hit_ratio_warning.doc: Warn when the shared buffers cache hit ratio falls below this fraction (0.0-1.0). Low ratios suggest shared_buffers is under-sized or working set is too large.

analyzer.checkpoint_frequency_warning_pe.doc: Warn when checkpoints occur more than this many times per hour. High frequency indicates max_wal_size is too small.

analyzer.idle_in_transaction_timeout_min.doc: Report sessions sitting in 'idle in transaction' longer than this. Long idle-in-tx sessions block vacuum and hold locks.

analyzer.index_bloat_threshold_pct.doc: Minimum bloat percentage (0-100) an index must exhibit before it becomes a REINDEX candidate.

analyzer.interval_seconds.doc: How often the analyzer runs its rule checks, in seconds. Lower values detect issues faster but add load.

analyzer.regression_lookback_days.doc: How many days of pg_stat_statements history the regression detector compares against.

analyzer.regression_threshold_pct.doc: Percent latency regression required before a query is flagged as a regressor. Set low for sensitive detection, higher to reduce noise.

analyzer.seq_scan_min_rows.doc: Minimum table row count before a sequential scan is reported as a finding. Below this, a seq scan is usually cheaper than an index lookup.

analyzer.slow_query_threshold_ms.doc: Queries with mean_exec_time above this are considered slow and become candidates for optimization findings.

analyzer.table_bloat_dead_tuple_pct.doc: Dead-tuple percentage threshold for reporting table bloat. Above this the analyzer emits a vacuum or repack finding.

analyzer.table_bloat_min_rows.doc: Minimum row count before a table is considered for bloat analysis. Tiny tables are noisy and not worth reporting.

analyzer.unused_index_window_days.doc: An index must be unused (idx_scan=0) for at least this many days before the analyzer suggests dropping it. Default 7 days.
analyzer.unused_index_window_days.warning: Setting this too low can recommend dropping indexes that support infrequent but critical queries (e.g. monthly reports).

analyzer.work_mem_promotion_threshold.doc: How many active work_mem hints a single role must accumulate before the analyzer recommends promoting work_mem at the role level via ALTER ROLE. Set 0 to disable the advisor.

analyzer.xid_wraparound_critical.doc: Transaction age at which the analyzer escalates to critical severity. Normally ~1.5 billion — past this autovacuum will force emergency wraparound runs.
analyzer.xid_wraparound_critical.warning: If this is hit, autovacuum enters aggressive mode and the database may eventually refuse writes.

analyzer.xid_wraparound_warning.doc: Transaction age at which the analyzer emits a wraparound warning. Normally ~200 million.

api.listen_addr.doc: Address the REST API and dashboard HTTP server binds to (host:port). Use :8080 to listen on all interfaces.

databases[].analyzer_interval_seconds.doc: Per-database analyzer interval override. Zero falls back to defaults.analyzer_interval_seconds.

databases[].collector_interval_seconds.doc: Per-database collector interval override. Zero falls back to defaults.collector_interval_seconds.

databases[].database.doc: Database name on the target instance.

databases[].host.doc: Hostname or IP of this fleet database.

databases[].max_connections.doc: Maximum connections the sidecar pgx pool opens to this database. Falls back to defaults.max_connections when zero.

databases[].name.doc: Logical name for this database in fleet mode. Must be unique across the fleet.

databases[].password.doc: Password for the login role. Encrypted at rest using encryption_key when persisted to the meta database.

databases[].port.doc: TCP port of this fleet database.

databases[].sslmode.doc: libpq sslmode for this connection (disable, prefer, require, verify-ca, verify-full).

databases[].tags.doc: Free-form tags used to group databases for fleet operations (e.g. tier=prod, team=payments).

databases[].trust_level.doc: Per-database override of the global trust.level. Useful for running a pilot database in autonomous while the fleet stays in advisory.

databases[].user.doc: Login role the sidecar uses to connect to this fleet database.

defaults.analyzer_interval_seconds.doc: Default analyzer interval applied to fleet databases that don't set their own value.

defaults.collector_interval_seconds.doc: Default collector interval applied to fleet databases that don't set their own value.

defaults.max_connections.doc: Default max_connections applied to fleet databases that don't set their own value.

defaults.trust_level.doc: Default trust level applied to fleet databases. Falls back to the global trust.level when unset.

encryption_key.doc: Passphrase used to encrypt sensitive fleet-mode fields (per-database passwords). Rotate via the key-rotation runbook.

llm.api_key.doc: Authentication token for the LLM provider. Prefer sourcing from environment via ${LLM_API_KEY} rather than committing literal values.

llm.context_budget_tokens.doc: Maximum tokens attached as context (schema, stats, plans) to a single LLM request. Prevents oversized prompts from busting the model context window.

llm.cooldown_seconds.doc: Minimum seconds between two LLM requests. Rate-limits the sidecar so it cannot burst the provider during a busy cycle.

llm.enabled.doc: Global enable for LLM-assisted analysis (planner explanations, recommendation narratives). When disabled, pg_sage falls back to deterministic heuristics only.

llm.endpoint.doc: LLM provider base URL. Supports OpenAI-compatible chat-completions endpoints. Can be left blank to use the vendor default.

llm.model.doc: Model identifier sent to the provider (e.g. gpt-4o-mini). Must support JSON-mode responses for structured outputs.

llm.optimizer_llm.api_key.doc: API key for the optimizer LLM. Prefer env-var substitution over literal values.

llm.optimizer_llm.cooldown_seconds.doc: Minimum seconds between two optimizer LLM requests.

llm.optimizer_llm.enabled.doc: Enable the dedicated reasoning-tier LLM used by the index optimizer. When false the optimizer falls back to the general llm.* client.

llm.optimizer_llm.endpoint.doc: Base URL for the optimizer LLM provider. Can differ from the general llm.endpoint when the reasoning tier lives on another provider.

llm.optimizer_llm.fallback_to_general.doc: If true, fall back to the general llm.* client when the optimizer LLM is unavailable or over budget.

llm.optimizer_llm.max_output_tokens.doc: Upper bound on output tokens accepted from the optimizer LLM. Truncates runaway responses.

llm.optimizer_llm.model.doc: Reasoning-tier model identifier (e.g. o1-mini). Must return JSON when requested.

llm.optimizer_llm.timeout_seconds.doc: Per-request timeout for optimizer LLM calls. Reasoning models typically need longer timeouts than chat models.

llm.optimizer_llm.token_budget_daily.doc: Daily token cap for the optimizer LLM — independent of the general llm.token_budget_daily so reasoning spend can be tracked separately.

llm.timeout_seconds.doc: HTTP request timeout for each LLM call. Low values protect the analyzer cycle from slow providers; high values tolerate cold-start latency.

llm.token_budget_daily.doc: Soft daily cap on total tokens (input + output) the sidecar will spend on LLM requests. Once exceeded the LLM is skipped until the next UTC day.

meta_db.doc: DSN of the metadata database used in fleet mode to persist cross-target state. Blank in standalone mode.

mode.doc: Operating mode: extension, standalone, or fleet. Standalone connects to one PostgreSQL target; fleet manages many databases from one sidecar.

oauth.client_id.doc: OAuth client ID issued by the provider when the pg_sage application was registered.

oauth.client_secret.doc: OAuth client secret issued by the provider. Source from an environment variable in production.

oauth.default_role.doc: Role assigned to newly authenticated users when no role mapping rule matches.

oauth.enabled.doc: Enable OAuth single sign-on for the sidecar UI. When false, the UI falls back to local auth.

oauth.issuer_url.doc: OIDC issuer URL used to discover the provider's authorization and token endpoints.

oauth.provider.doc: OAuth provider identifier. Supported values include google, github, okta, and generic OIDC.

oauth.redirect_url.doc: Absolute URL the provider redirects to after authentication. Must match the URI registered at the provider.

postgres.database.doc: Target database name the sidecar connects to. Ignored when database_url is set.

postgres.database_url.doc: Full libpq connection URL. When set, overrides host/port/user/password/database/sslmode.

postgres.host.doc: Hostname or IP of the target PostgreSQL instance. Ignored when database_url is set.

postgres.max_connections.doc: Maximum connections the sidecar pgx pool will open to this target. Keep well below max_connections on the server.

postgres.password.doc: Password for the sidecar login role. Prefer environment-variable substitution or a secrets manager over committing literal values.

postgres.port.doc: TCP port of the target PostgreSQL instance. Ignored when database_url is set.

postgres.sslmode.doc: libpq sslmode string (disable, allow, prefer, require, verify-ca, verify-full). Use verify-full in production.

postgres.user.doc: PostgreSQL login role used by the sidecar. Requires pg_read_all_stats plus write access to the sage schema.

safety.backoff_consecutive_skips.doc: After this many consecutive skipped cycles (e.g. CPU ceiling hit repeatedly), enter dormant mode and slow the cadence until load subsides.

safety.cpu_ceiling_pct.doc: Skip cycles when database-host CPU exceeds this percent (1-100). Prevents pg_sage from adding load during incidents.

safety.ddl_timeout_seconds.doc: Timeout applied to DDL actions (CREATE INDEX, ALTER TABLE) executed by the executor. Per-statement ceiling, not per-cycle.

safety.disk_pressure_threshold_pct.doc: When database data volume usage exceeds this percent, skip write-producing actions. Prevents adding indexes to a near-full volume.

safety.dormant_interval_seconds.doc: Cycle interval (seconds) used while in dormant mode — typically much larger than the normal interval so the sidecar wakes rarely while the target is stressed.

safety.lock_timeout_ms.doc: lock_timeout applied to connections running DDL or ANALYZE. Must be > 0 for ANALYZE actions in v0.8.5 — autovacuum can hold a ShareUpdateExclusiveLock indefinitely.
safety.lock_timeout_ms.warning: Zero disables the timeout entirely and is refused by the executor for ANALYZE actions.

safety.query_timeout_ms.doc: statement_timeout applied to every collector and analyzer query issued by the sidecar. Protects the target from runaway introspection.

trust.cascade_cooldown_cycles.doc: If N consecutive cycles each propose new actions, throttle to observe impact. Prevents storm-like cascades of small changes.

trust.level.doc: Autonomy tier. observation = log only; advisory = queue actions for approval; autonomous = execute safe/moderate actions directly.
trust.level.warning: Changing to autonomous enables DDL execution without human review.

trust.maintenance_window.doc: Cron-like window (e.g. 'Sun 02:00-06:00') during which heavy maintenance actions like ANALYZE on large tables or REINDEX are permitted.

trust.ramp_start.doc: RFC3339 timestamp when the trust ramp began. Auto-persisted on first startup if empty. Used to gate newly-supported actions behind a soak period.

trust.rollback_cooldown_days.doc: After a rollback, the same action family is blocked for this many days to prevent flap. Default 3 days.

trust.rollback_threshold_pct.doc: Latency regression percentage (0-100) that triggers automatic rollback of a recently applied action. Lower = more sensitive.

trust.rollback_window_minutes.doc: How many minutes after applying an action pg_sage watches for regressions before considering it stable.

trust.tier3_high_risk.doc: Enable tier-3 actions classified as high risk. Ignored outside fleet mode — standalone always forces this to false.
trust.tier3_high_risk.warning: High-risk actions can destabilize production — enable only with a reviewed rollback plan.

trust.tier3_moderate.doc: Enable tier-3 actions classified as moderate risk (e.g. VACUUM FULL on small tables). Requires trust.level=autonomous.
trust.tier3_moderate.warning: Moderate actions can briefly lock tables.

trust.tier3_safe.doc: Enable tier-3 actions classified as safe (e.g. CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on small tables). Requires trust.level=autonomous.

tuner.analyze_cooldown_minutes.doc: Minimum minutes between tuner-initiated ANALYZE runs on the same table. Also respects autovacuum last_analyze as a recent-run signal.

tuner.analyze_maintenance_threshold_mb.doc: Tables larger than this many MB can only be ANALYZEd during the configured maintenance_window. Default 1024 (1GB).

tuner.analyze_max_table_mb.doc: Tables larger than this many MB are never auto-ANALYZEd — an advisory finding is emitted instead for operator review.
tuner.analyze_max_table_mb.warning: Default 10240 (10GB). Raise with care — ANALYZE on TB-scale tables can saturate I/O.

tuner.analyze_timeout_ms.doc: statement_timeout for ANALYZE actions. Default 600000 (10 minutes). Exceeding this marks the action failed_timeout and extends the cooldown.

tuner.enabled.doc: Master switch for the per-query tuner. When false, no hints are written and Tune() is a no-op — the analyzer still runs.

tuner.hint_retirement_days.doc: Hints older than this many days are retired unconditionally, regardless of current query behavior. Safety net against stale hint accumulation.

tuner.llm_enabled.doc: Allow the tuner to call the LLM for hint prescription when deterministic rules do not produce a recommendation. Budget-gated by llm.token_budget_daily.

tuner.max_concurrent_analyze.doc: Hard cap on concurrent ANALYZE actions across the entire sidecar process (shared semaphore — fleet-wide, not per-database). Default 1.

tuner.min_query_calls.doc: Require at least this many pg_stat_statements.calls before the tuner will act on a query. Filters out one-offs.

tuner.nested_loop_row_threshold.doc: Minimum actual-row count on a nested-loop inner that triggers the bad-nested-loop symptom. Smaller values increase sensitivity.

tuner.parallel_min_table_rows.doc: Threshold for suggesting parallel-enable hints on large sequential scans. Tables smaller than this are assumed not to benefit from parallelism.

tuner.plan_time_ratio.doc: When plan_time / exec_time exceeds this ratio, the tuner flags the query as plan-time-dominated and considers prepared-statement hints.

tuner.revalidation_explain_timeout_ms.doc: statement_timeout applied to EXPLAIN queries issued by the revalidation loop. Queries that cannot be explained in time are deferred.

tuner.revalidation_interval_hours.doc: How often the hint revalidation loop runs. Set to 0 to disable the loop entirely. Default 24 hours.

tuner.revalidation_keep_ratio.doc: During cost comparison, hinted plan cost must be at most this ratio of the unhinted plan to keep the hint. Default 1.2 (hinted <= 120% of unhinted).

tuner.revalidation_rollback_ratio.doc: When hinted-plan cost exceeds unhinted by this ratio, the hint is marked broken and rolled back. Default 0.8 (hinted >= 125% of unhinted).

tuner.stale_stats_age_minutes.doc: Both manual and autovacuum last_analyze must be older than this many minutes before the table is considered stale. Default 60.

tuner.stale_stats_estimate_skew.doc: Ratio ActualRows / PlanRows above which a plan node is considered row-estimate skewed. Default 10 — same threshold as the bad-nested-loop check.

tuner.stale_stats_mod_ratio.doc: Fraction of rows modified since last ANALYZE (n_mod_since_analyze / reltuples) required to treat a table as stale. Default 0.1 = 10%.

tuner.verify_after_apply.doc: Run the daily hint revalidation loop. Removes hints that have become redundant, harmful, or broken. Requires pg_hint_plan.
tuner.verify_after_apply.warning: v0.8.5 activates a real background cycle here — upgrade note: if you want v0.8.4 inert behavior, set this to false before upgrading.

tuner.work_mem_max_mb.doc: Maximum per-query work_mem (MB) the tuner is allowed to prescribe via Set(work_mem) hints. Ceiling prevents runaway memory suggestions.
